gloss.en: to stop working, usually at an older age

words.example: He plans to retire next year after thirty years.

gloss.en: having stopped working because of age

words.example: My grandfather is a retired teacher.

gloss.en: thinking about a past event or time

words.example: In retrospect, I should have taken the job.

gloss.en: the act of joining a country together again

words.example: The reunification of the country brought great joy.

gloss.en: to join a country or group together again

words.example: Many people hoped to reunify the divided nation.

gloss.en: to change a plan or opinion after new information

words.example: We had to revise our plans after the news.

gloss.en: to write something again in a different way

words.example: The teacher asked me to rewrite my essay.

gloss.en: ready to be eaten, fully grown

words.example: These bananas are ripe and ready to eat.

gloss.en: an increase in amount, number, or level

words.example: There has been a sharp rise in prices.

gloss.en: the chance that something bad may happen

words.example: Smoking increases the risk of heart disease.

gloss.en: the edge or side of a road

words.example: We stopped at the roadside to rest.

gloss.en: a machine that can do tasks by itself

words.example: These cars are built by robots.

gloss.en: a small round loaf of bread for one person

words.example: I had soup and a bread roll for lunch.

gloss.en: having gentle hills that rise and fall

gloss.en: connected with love or a love relationship

gloss.en: very strong thick string

words.example: He tied the boat with a thick rope.

gloss.en: old and bad, no longer fresh

words.example: Throw away the rotten apples.

gloss.en: not smooth or even on the surface

gloss.en: a line of people or things next to each other

gloss.en: connected with a king or queen

gloss.en: things you throw away because you don't need them

words.example: Please put the rubbish in the bin.

gloss.en: in a way that is not polite

words.example: He spoke rudely to the waiter.

gloss.en: a team ball game using an oval ball

words.example: They played a game of rugby on Saturday.

gloss.en: a statement of what you must or must not do

words.example: You must follow the rules of the game.

gloss.en: a person who has left home secretly

words.example: The police found the young runaway.

gloss.en: the strip where planes take off and land

words.example: The plane waited on the runway.

gloss.en: the feeling of being sad

words.example: She felt great sadness when he left.

gloss.en: a trip to see wild animals, often in Africa

words.example: We went on a safari to see lions.

gloss.en: something that protects against harm or risk

words.example: Passwords are a safeguard against theft.

gloss.en: in a safe way, without danger

words.example: They arrived home safely after the trip.

gloss.en: the state of being safe from danger

words.example: The children can play here in safety.

gloss.en: a person who works on a ship

words.example: The sailor worked hard on the ship.

gloss.en: a man whose job is selling things

words.example: The salesman showed us a new car.

gloss.en: a large pink fish that is eaten as food

words.example: We had grilled salmon for dinner.

gloss.en: very small grains of rock found on beaches

words.example: The children played in the sand.

gloss.en: a light open shoe worn in warm weather

words.example: She wore sandals on the beach.

gloss.en: a deep round pan with a handle for cooking

words.example: Heat the soup in a saucepan.

gloss.en: a small plate placed under a cup

words.example: She put the cup on the saucer.

gloss.en: a quick look through a document

gloss.en: frightened or afraid of something

words.example: The child was scared of the dark.

gloss.en: frightening, making you feel afraid

words.example: We watched a scary film last night.

gloss.en: to throw or spread things over a wide area

words.example: She scattered seeds across the garden.

gloss.en: having beautiful natural views

words.example: We took the scenic road by the coast.

gloss.en: a person who goes to the same school as you

gloss.en: a person who teaches in a school

words.example: My mother is a schoolteacher.

gloss.en: connected with or involving science

words.example: They made an important scientific discovery.

gloss.en: to speak angrily to someone for doing wrong

words.example: The teacher scolded the boy for being late.

gloss.en: the number of points won in a game

words.example: The final score was three to one.

gloss.en: the written words of a play or film

gloss.en: art made by carving or shaping a material

words.example: The museum has a famous marble sculpture.

gloss.en: one of the four parts of the year

gloss.en: the salty water of the sea

words.example: Seawater tastes very salty.

gloss.en: a plant that grows in the sea

words.example: Some people eat seaweed in their soup.

gloss.en: already owned or used by someone else

gloss.en: known by only a few people, kept hidden

gloss.en: in a way that is hidden from others

gloss.en: protection against attack or danger

gloss.en: the small part of a plant that grows new plants

words.example: She planted some flower seeds in spring.

gloss.en: where customers serve themselves

gloss.en: caring only about yourself, not others

gloss.en: not joined to something else, on its own

gloss.en: a set of TV programmes with the same theme

words.example: I watched the new series last night.

gloss.en: bad, dangerous, or worrying

gloss.en: in a serious or severe way

words.example: He was seriously injured in the crash.

gloss.en: a computer that supplies data to others

words.example: The website is hosted on a fast server.

gloss.en: a system that provides what the public needs

words.example: The bus service is very good in this city.

gloss.en: a group of similar things that go together

words.example: She bought a set of six chairs.

gloss.en: the place where something happens

words.example: The hotel is in a beautiful setting.

gloss.en: the state of being male or female

words.example: How can you tell the sex of a fish?

gloss.en: full of shadows, dark and unclear

gloss.en: an act of moving something quickly side to side

words.example: She gave him a shake to wake him up.

gloss.en: so bad that it makes you feel ashamed

words.example: It was a shameful way to treat a friend.

gloss.en: one part of something divided among people

words.example: Everyone gets a fair share of the money.

gloss.en: having an edge that can cut easily

words.example: Be careful, the knife is very sharp.

gloss.en: to cut hair off the skin with a razor

words.example: He likes to shave every morning.

gloss.en: a large piece of cloth used on a bed

words.example: I changed the sheets on the bed.

gloss.en: a flat board on a wall for placing things

gloss.en: the hard outer part of an egg, nut, or animal

words.example: We collected shells on the beach.

gloss.en: a chief police officer in a US county

words.example: The sheriff arrested the thief.

gloss.en: to give out or reflect bright light

words.example: The sun shone brightly in the sky.

gloss.en: smooth and bright, reflecting light

words.example: She has long shiny black hair.

gloss.en: to shake slightly because you are cold or afraid

words.example: The cold wind made her shiver.

gloss.en: a person who is buying things in a shop

words.example: The shops were full of busy shoppers.

gloss.en: a small sea animal with a shell, eaten as food

words.example: We ate fried shrimp for dinner.

gloss.en: not open, closed

words.example: The shop was shut when we arrived.

gloss.en: nervous about meeting and talking to people

words.example: He is too shy to speak in class.

gloss.en: the state of being ill

words.example: He missed work because of sickness.

gloss.en: a path beside a road for people to walk on

words.example: We walked along the sidewalk to the shop.

gloss.en: the ability to see

words.example: She lost her sight in an accident.

gloss.en: a movement or sound that gives information

words.example: He gave a signal for the car to stop.

gloss.en: a sign showing the direction and distance

words.example: The signpost pointed to the next town.

gloss.en: with little or no sound; not speaking

words.example: The whole room became silent.

gloss.en: showing a lack of thought or sense

gloss.en: the state of being alike but not the same

words.example: There is a similarity between the two photos.

gloss.en: in almost the same way

words.example: Both cars are similarly priced.

gloss.en: to make something easier or simpler

gloss.en: in an easy or plain way; just

gloss.en: to go down below the surface of water

words.example: The old ship began to sink slowly.

gloss.en: the frame of bones in a body

gloss.en: the outer layer that covers the body

gloss.en: a very tall city building

gloss.en: the natural state of rest with eyes closed

gloss.en: the part of clothing that covers the arm

gloss.en: a thin flat piece cut from something

gloss.en: a little; to a small degree

words.example: This shirt is slightly bigger than that one.

gloss.en: not moving or done quickly

words.example: The train was very slow today.

gloss.en: clean and neat; well dressed

words.example: He looked smart in his new suit.

gloss.en: a person who smokes cigarettes

words.example: My uncle is a heavy smoker.

gloss.en: flat and even, without rough areas

gloss.en: a sudden burst of air from the nose

gloss.en: a storm with heavy snow and strong wind

gloss.en: in a way related to society or meeting people

gloss.en: the programs that a computer uses

gloss.en: hard and firm, not liquid or gas

gloss.en: on some occasions but not always
